title: C.G. Jung — Psychological Types (1921) — PRIMARY TEXT DEEP INGEST

concepts.title: Introversion/Extraversion: The Direction of Libido
concepts.description: fundamental attitude determining where psychic energy flows; not social comfort but directional bias; introverts accumulate libido internally (depth); extraverts discharge libido externally (breadth); both necessary; neurotic patterns differ by attitude (introvert withdraws, extravert acts frantically)

concepts.title: Superior Function: The Tyranny and Necessity of the Dominant
concepts.description: the function through which consciousness primarily organizes reality; highly differentiated; reliable but rigid; identification with superior = possession; neurosis through over-extension; compensation generates opposite unconscious content

concepts.title: Compensation Principle: Automatic Psychological Balancing
concepts.description: one-sided consciousness generates equal opposite unconscious content; mechanical, not corrective; the more extreme the conscious position, the more extreme the unconscious opposite; understanding prevents fighting the eruption

concepts.title: Thinking Function: Logic and Discrimination
concepts.description: discriminates true/false; applies logic to determine reality; external (extraverted thinking: logic applied to facts) or internal (introverted thinking: logic applied to principles); superior form brilliant and reliable; inferior form tortured and reactive; primary function in philosophy, science, systems

concepts.title: Feeling Function: Value and Meaning
concepts.description: discriminates valuable/valueless; determines what matters; external (extraverted feeling: values applied to relationships) or internal (introverted feeling: values applied internally as authenticity); superior form creates genuine connection; inferior form reactive and overwhelming

concepts.title: Sensation Function: Concrete Fact Perception
concepts.description: discriminates present/absent; perceives concrete reality; external (extraverted sensation: facts engaged externally) or internal (introverted sensation: subtle body sensation); superior form grounds in practical reality; inferior form overwhelmed by sensation or disconnected from it

concepts.title: Intuition Function: Pattern and Possibility Perception
concepts.description: discriminates becoming/stable; perceives pattern and implication; external (extraverted intuition: possibilities perceived externally) or internal (introverted intuition: archetypal meaning perceived internally); superior form visionary; inferior form paranoid or obsessive

concepts.title: Transcendent Function: The Only Mechanism for Integration
concepts.description: the psyche's ability to hold contradictions without collapse; operates through symbol, not logic; generates meaning by containing paradox; the function through which the Self becomes conscious; accessible only when the superior function releases its grip

concepts.title: Symbol as Living Form: Distinction from Metaphor and Allegory
concepts.description: contains paradox without resolution; alive because it grows with the viewer; regenerative; generates meaning through the tension it holds; difference from metaphor (dead comparison) and allegory (represents something else); the language of the unconscious and of spirit

concepts.title: Inferior Function: Neurotic Gateway and Path to Wholeness
concepts.description: opposite to superior; undifferentiated; primitive; source of neurosis through possession; yet the gateway to the unconscious and the Self; development is impossible; integration requires conscious relationship (not elimination or perfection); eruption signals compensation at work

concepts.title: Anima/Animus: The Inner Feminine and Masculine
concepts.description: the contrasexual dimension of the psyche; not opposite sex but the opposite-coded functions within you; shaped by biology, culture, parental imago; operates through projection in relationships; integration requires withdrawing projections and meeting the inner figure

concepts.title: Libido: Psychic Energy as Universal Life Force
concepts.description: not sexual drive but psychic energy itself; flows into whatever commands attention; concentrates, depletes, regenerates; superior function is primary channel; lifespan redistribution (first half: ego-building; second half: meaning-making); blocked libido produces neurotic symptoms

concepts.title: Consciousness vs. Unconscious: The Divided Psyche
concepts.description: consciousness is thin film on vast ocean; unconscious autonomous and often opposed to conscious intention; personal unconscious (recoverable) vs. collective unconscious (inherited); dialogue through dream, symbol, active imagination

concepts.title: Differentiation vs. Undifferentiation: The Work of Becoming Conscious
concepts.description: refinement and separation of functions so each operates with clarity; undifferentiated functions blurry and reactive; integration requires more than differentiation—requires conscious relationship with the opposite

concepts.title: Neurotic Patterns by Type: The Signature Neuroses
concepts.description: each type has characteristic neurotic shape; thinking-type neurosis: possession by logic, starvation of feeling; feeling-type: possession by values, alienation from logic; sensation-type: possession by concrete, starvation of meaning; intuitive-type: possession by possibility, disconnection from reality; inferior function eruptions are compensation

concepts.title: Jung and Vedic Psychology: Parallel Structures of Self and Consciousness
concepts.description: Jung's Self functionally identical to Vedic Atman (ultimate self beyond ego); prana = libido; three bodies (waking/dream/deep sleep) parallel type theory; samadhi = active imagination; integration in both systems requires moving beyond ego-consciousness

concepts.title: Jung and Taoism: The Psychology of Wu Wei and Complementary Opposites
concepts.description: Tao = Self (unknowable center generating all opposites); yin/yang = compensation principle; wu wei (non-forcing action) = active imagination; watercourse way = natural flow of psyche; integration as alignment rather than force

concepts.title: Jung and Japanese Philosophy: Type, Emptiness, and the Way

concepts.title: Type Application to Historical Analysis: How Consciousness Shapes Epochs

concepts.title: Type Classification System: The Eight Types Mapped
concepts.description: 2 attitudes (intro/extra) × 4 functions (T/F/S/I) = 8 types; each type's natural habitat, strength, weakness, neurotic pattern, examples; auxiliary function as secondary structure; type and life pattern (extraverts easier first half, introverts easier second half); type and vulnerability (strength = greatest vulnerability)

concepts.title: Consciousness and Ego: The Constructed Self

title: Behavioral Economics and Decision Science (Thaler, Misbehaving, 2015)

concepts.title: Homo Economicus vs. Homo Sapiens
concepts.description: The core tension: economic theory's rational-actor fiction vs. how humans actually decide; why the Econ model persists despite empirical failure; systematic ways humans diverge

concepts.title: Loss Aversion: Asymmetric Valuation
concepts.description: Foundational mechanism: losses hurt ~2x more than equivalent gains feel good; value function shape (concave for gains, convex for losses); evolutionary logic; applications across decision domains

concepts.title: Endowment Effect: Ownership Premium
concepts.description: Ownership inflates valuation 50-100%; WTA > WTP for identical items; four mechanisms: loss aversion activation, entitlement anchoring, identity blending, imaginative elaboration

concepts.title: Reference Dependence and Anchors
concepts.description: All value is reference-dependent; same outcome feels different by reference point; four mechanisms: status quo as default, expectations/entitlements, arbitrary anchoring, social comparison

concepts.title: Supposedly Irrelevant Factors (SIFs)
concepts.description: Factors Econ theory says irrelevant (sunk costs, fairness, transaction utility) but empirically matter; not irrational but reflect adaptive psychological features; examples: concert tickets, wage cuts, reference prices

concepts.title: Mental Accounting Framework
concepts.description: How people psychologically organize money into categories with different rules and loss thresholds; four dimensions: source, purpose, time horizon, loss threshold; creates artificial constraints despite nominal wealth

concepts.title: Transaction Utility: Bargains and Ripoffs
concepts.description: Pleasure/pain of deal independent of product quality; reference price determines transaction utility; fairness perception key; explains bundling, price transparency effects, negotiated pricing satisfaction

concepts.title: Sunk Cost Fallacy: Mechanisms and Entitlement
concepts.description: Why people continue bad investments/situations; three mechanisms: loss aversion, entitlement/obligation, identity/commitment signals; distinction between fallacy and legitimate commitment

concepts.title: Mental Budgets and Segregation
concepts.description: Money segregated into separate mental accounts creates artificial constraints; cash poverty despite nominal wealth; "pay yourself first" works through segregation not willpower

concepts.title: House Money Effect: Windfall Spending
concepts.description: Windfall money activates different spending rules than earned income; feels "free" to spend; weaker loss aversion; explains spending paradox

concepts.title: Nominal Illusion: Wage Frames
concepts.description: Same real outcome feels different by nominal frame (wage cut rejected; wage freeze accepted); determines fairness perception despite identical real outcome

concepts.title: Reference Price as Entitlement
concepts.description: Established price becomes psychological right; increases feel like violations; explains price stickiness, customer anger, fairness more than supply-demand

concepts.title: Planner vs. Doer: Dual Selves in Conflict
concepts.description: Two competing decision systems: cool Planner vs. hot impulsive Doer; time-inconsistent preferences; temporal discounting creates gap between intentions and execution

concepts.title: Present Bias and Hyperbolic Discounting
concepts.description: Steep discount rate for immediate future, equal for distant futures (hyperbolic, not exponential); generates procrastination, addiction, diet failures; sophisticated vs. naive discounters

concepts.title: Self-Control and Commitment Devices
concepts.description: Self-control is environmental design not willpower; commitment devices (autopay, accountability, penalties, habits) bind Doer to Planner's plans without willpower; removing choice more effective than increasing willpower

concepts.title: Fairness Norms in Pricing
concepts.description: Prices constrained by fairness norms more than supply-demand; cost visibility, profit margins, universal application determine fairness; firms don't maximize short-term profit if it violates fairness

concepts.title: Price Gouging and Emergency Pricing
concepts.description: Extreme fairness violation: exploiting desperation triggers moral outrage; firms choosing fairness-optimal over profit-optimal pricing because fairness violations destroy reputation

concepts.title: Preference Reversals: Selling vs. Buying
concepts.description: WTA > WTP for identical items; loss aversion + endowment effect create asymmetry; explains real estate, wage, insurance market gaps

concepts.title: Perceived Fairness and Preference Reversals
concepts.description: Frame (buying vs. selling) activates different fairness standards; same price fair in buying frame, unfair in selling frame; fairness frame-dependent not objective

concepts.title: Firm Reputation vs. Short-Term Profit
concepts.description: Profitable firms don't maximize short-term price; fairness violations destroy reputation worth more than gained profit; long-term profit maximization requires fairness constraints

concepts.title: Market Entry and Fair Dealing
concepts.description: New competitors face asymmetric fairness constraints: incumbents can violate fairness (reputation buffer); newcomers cannot; creates reputation equilibrium lock preventing entry

concepts.title: Narrow Framing: Daily Targets and Risk Aversion
concepts.description: Evaluating decisions in isolation produces excessive risk aversion; single coin flip feels risky; ten flips feel acceptable (identical EV); explains excessive trading, risk aversion, unrealistic targets

concepts.title: Myopic Loss Aversion: Portfolio Checking Frequency
concepts.description: Checking frequency determines behavior; daily feels too risky; annual feels acceptable (identical risk); explains equity premium puzzle (6% gap); solution: change evaluation horizon

concepts.title: Overconfidence in Markets
concepts.description: Traders overestimate skill despite incentives for accuracy; persists through attribution bias, weak feedback, recency bias; leads to excessive trading, concentrated bets, risk underestimation

concepts.title: Equity Premium Puzzle

concepts.title: Stock Market Bubbles and Overvaluation

concepts.title: Availability Heuristic and Fear

concepts.title: Anchoring and Adjustment

concepts.title: Sublime Hatred Paradox

concepts.title: Self-Denial Confers Cruelty
concepts.description: Three multiplicative threads: moral credit account licenses harshness; pride paradox converts humility into "one of the chosen, the salt of the earth"; responsibility dissolution enables "freedom to hate, bully, lie, torture, murder and betray without shame and remorse"

concepts.title: Unification Paradox
concepts.description: Movement promises to resolve incompleteness; instead manufactures deeper incompleteness via six mechanisms (dogma above reason, economic dependence, communal quarters, enforced participation, censorship, inculcated devotions); born-in members manufactured incomplete without prior frustrated self

concepts.title: Corporate Cohesion Inversion
concepts.description: Rising movements disrupt corporate cohesion because intact cohesion immunizes against recruitment; Phase 1 disruption; Phase 2 reconstruction (Soviet 1917-1936 reversal); divide-and-rule nuance: fragmented compact cohesion more stable than full atomization

concepts.title: The Soul Concept: Multivalent Definition
concepts.description: soul across traditions (Plato, Christian, Hindu atman, indigenous); Jungian soul; soul as authentic self imprisoned by trauma

concepts.title: Intermediary Realm: Mundus Imaginalis
concepts.description: real but non-material reality; imaginal realm across traditions; sanctuary function; healing work in imaginal space

concepts.title: Presence and Personhood
concepts.description: presence as mark of personhood; trauma's dissociation destroying presence; restoration pathway; two-worlds reintegration

concepts.title: Innocence and Its Loss
concepts.description: sacred vulnerability; trauma's theft of innocence; divine child archetype; grief of innocence lost; harrowing as return

concepts.title: The Threshold Concept: Liminal Space
concepts.description: liminality as vulnerable transition; healing as series of thresholds; witness's role containing chaos; initiation rites across cultures

concepts.title: Symbolic Thinking vs. Concrete Thinking
concepts.description: symbolic capacity; trauma's fragmentation of symbolic function; integration pathway; creative practice dependence on symbolic thinking

concepts.title: Regression as Path to Progression
concepts.description: regression as necessary for healing; appearing as failure; three-stage pathway (defend → regress → integrate)

concepts.title: Divine/Human Relationship in Suffering
concepts.description: sacred encountered through suffering; wound as gateway; theodicy of presence; numinous in crisis

concepts.title: The Wounded Healer
concepts.description: archetypal pattern across cultures (Chiron, shamanic wound, Christ, Jung's analyst); wound as authority source; transformation into gift

concepts.title: Reference Dependence in Identity Architecture
concepts.description: identity anchored to reference points from early relational experience; "invisible crosshairs" framework; loss aversion explains why moving from reference point feels catastrophic

concepts.title: Mental Accounting and Shadow Partitioning
concepts.description: nervous system runs multiple incompatible accounting systems; why intellectual knowledge doesn't update procedural patterns

concepts.title: Sunk Costs and Character Persistence
concepts.description: why character patterns persist despite conscious recognition; three mechanisms overcoming sunk cost attachment (crisis, new identity building, grief reframing)

concepts.title: Energy Dancer: Working with Energy States as a Living Practice
concepts.description: Aware Ego consciousness position; access to all available energies rather than being trapped in one primary energy; five capacities; practical outcome of Voice Dialogue

concepts.title: Language as Technology
concepts.description: language as shaped tool; bakes in implicit frameworks; divides continuous reality; Construct-Aware stage as where this becomes visceral predicament

concepts.title: Controlled Folly
concepts.description: Castaneda/Don Juan: Unitive-stage engagement with meaningful work without needing it to matter cosmically; full engagement + zero cosmic attachment; liberation from need-to-matter

concepts.title: Two-Worlds Framework: The Split Between Matter and Spirit That Trauma Creates
concepts.description: Kalsched's binocular consciousness model; trauma severs connection between material self and spiritual/authentic self; two-self organization (hollow material self + imprisoned authentic self); transitional space as bridge for reconnection; cross-domain: Advaita non-dual realization, sacred/profane in ritual

concepts.title: Generational Survival Pattern Cycling: The Civilizational Feedback Loop
concepts.description: each generation's adaptive survival responses become the constraints the next generation inherits; Boomer→GenX→Millennial→GenZ cycling of blind spots; trauma becomes worldview becomes invisible assumption; intergenerational consciousness as the only counter-mechanism

concepts.title: Primal Pain: The Foundational Wound
concepts.description: emotional pain from unmet basic needs that exceeds infant integration capacity; encoded at cellular level; pre-verbal and inaccessible to talk therapy

concepts.title: Imprinting: How Early Experience Becomes Biology
concepts.description: nervous system encoding at cellular level; distinct from narrative memory; immutable structure; critical periods ages 0-10 maximum susceptibility

concepts.title: Critical Periods and Imprinting Susceptibility
concepts.description: maximum plasticity ages 0-10; severe force required for new imprinting post-10; damage frontloaded; prevents later reliving trauma from imprinting as deeply

concepts.title: Compounding: How Early Imprints Are Reinforced by Similar Events
concepts.description: similar events across development reinforce and crystallize imprints; transforms incident into learned pattern; explains why some develop severe pathology from mild triggers

concepts.title: Gating Mechanism: Endorphin-Mediated Repression
concepts.description: endorphin production proportional to pain blocks pain signal transmission at thalamic gate; active ongoing process requiring neurochemical maintenance; successful but costly

concepts.title: Emotional vs. Narrative Memory: Distinct Storage Systems
concepts.description: limbic/brainstem emotional memory pre-verbal and inaccessible to narrative; cortical narrative memory revisable through talk; systems don't automatically communicate

concepts.title: The Three Lines of Consciousness: Brainstem, Limbic, and Cortical Storage
concepts.description: First Line (survival, pre-verbal, pure anxiety); Second Line (emotional, imagery-based); Third Line (cognitive, narrative). Each stores trauma independently; requires level-specific access. Talk therapy works on third line only

concepts.title: Somatization: How Psychological Imprints Become Physical Symptoms
concepts.description: imprints encoded in tissue; nervous system maintains dysregulation at tissue site; real symptoms without structural pathology; reflects gated imprints expressed somatically

concepts.title: The Reticular Activating System: Chronic Hypervigilance
concepts.description: RAS maintains baseline hypervigilance in response to gated imprints; nervous system perpetually receives signal of threat despite absence of danger; drives sleep disruption, anxiety, startle response

concepts.title: The Anxiety Hierarchy: Paths to Psychosis
concepts.description: pure anxiety (Level 1) → phobia (Level 2) → OCD (Level 3) → psychosis (Level 4). Each level is attempt to contain First Line material through vocabulary of higher line. Defense failure produces escalation

concepts.title: Birth Prototype: Personality Determined at Birth
concepts.description: birth outcome (struggle succeeds = sympathetic; struggle fails = parasympathetic) imprints prototype that governs autonomic baseline, personality type, disease susceptibility for life. Sympathetic = high energy, approach, heart disease. Parasympathetic = low energy, withdrawal, cancer

concepts.title: The Prototype in Action: Birth Imprint Manifesting as Adult Patterns
concepts.description: sympathetic prototype: initiative, striving, domination, cardiovascular risk. Parasympathetic prototype: withdrawal, acceptance, passivity, cancer risk. Prototype shows as personality that feels like "who I am"

concepts.title: Depression as Parasympathetic State
concepts.description: not disease but nervous system mode; constitutional parasympathetic = depressive features (low energy, resignation). Defensive depression = suppression effort against rising material

concepts.title: Acting Out: Neurotic Mastery and Behavioral Reenactment
concepts.description: imprints drive unconscious behavior seeking to recreate and master original trauma; repetition compulsion; person experiences as personality/preference; acting out felt as free choice

concepts.title: Neurotic vs. Authentic Self: The Personality Before and After Reliving
concepts.description: neurotic self = organized around imprints, defended, compulsive. Authentic self = available post-reliving, acts from preference, freed from compulsion

concepts.title: Reliving as Healing: Full Autonomic Discharge and Integration
concepts.description: reactivate original autonomic state while completing interrupted response; vital signs spike (pulse 200+, BP 220+, temp +2-3°F); nervous system experiences completion and resolution. Only mechanism that integrates pre-verbal imprints

concepts.title: Integration After Reliving: Post-Healing Reorganization
concepts.description: post-reliving: energy becomes available, behavioral patterns loosen, relationships reorganize, authentic preference emerges, health often improves. Timeline: days-to-years for full integration

concepts.title: Trauma
concepts.description: (if exists) — add section on imprinting vs. narrative memory distinction

concepts.title: Repression
concepts.description: (if exists) — add neurochemical mechanism; endorphin gating

concepts.title: Autonomic Nervous System
concepts.description: add sympathetic/parasympathetic personality typing

concepts.title: Personality Development
concepts.description: add birth prototype determination; critical periods

concepts.title: Anxiety Disorders
concepts.description: add hierarchy progression model

concepts.title: Behavioral Modification
concepts.description: add level-specificity caveat

concepts.title: Psychosomatic Illness
concepts.description: update with imprint-to-tissue mechanism

concepts.title: Immanent vs. External Reflection: Methodological Foundation
concepts.description: Psychology becomes possible only through immanent reflection; external reflection destroys access to soul. Gigerenzer's foundational methodological divide

concepts.title: Two Senses of Meaning: Ontological vs. Utilitarian
concepts.description: Meaning as intrinsic logical character vs. meaning as utility. Modernity reversed this distinction, destroying psychology's epistemic ground

concepts.title: Soul Events as Events of Meaning
concepts.description: Psychological phenomena as self-referential events. Presupposition that enables psychology as discipline; psychology requires treating all phenomena as intrinsically meaningful

concepts.title: Fairytale and Archetypal Methodology: Consciousness Generating Reality
concepts.description: Gigerenzer's methodology for reading archetypes as logical structures that consciousness actualizes. Same archetypal event reads as initiation OR crime depending on stance

concepts.title: The Sacrifice of Isaac as Watershed: Consciousness Divide
concepts.description: Single moment where Western consciousness irreversibly diverged into two streams. Archaic (human sacrifice) vs. Modern (faith test substitution). Pivot point from which to recognize modernity's position

concepts.title: Psychology as Modern Discipline: Emergence and Necessity
concepts.description: Psychology could not exist before modernity. Jung's insight: psychology is response to loss of external forms (myth, ritual, religion), not natural discovery

concepts.title: The Child as Logical Form of Consciousness
concepts.description: "The child" as consciousness syntax, not developmental stage. Innocence, unwoundedness, wholeness, embeddedness in meaning. Modernity's unprecedented settlement in this form

concepts.title: Methodological Violence: Killing the Child as Entrance to Psychology
concepts.description: No bridge from ordinary to psychological consciousness. Requires radical translocation via violence. Lasciate ogni speranza: hope must be abandoned

concepts.title: The Three Stances Toward the Animus
concepts.description: Phenomenology of consciousness encountering its other: First (anima captivated), Second (anima terrorized), Third (anima triumphant). Each complete yet carrying others sublated

title: Psychological Development: Shadow Through Sacrifice (10 MEDIUM)

concepts.title: Shadow as First Psychological Inwardization
concepts.description: One's own other; apprentice-piece; violent alchemical work; mortificatio, putrefaction, burning

concepts.title: Anima/Animus and the Masterpiece of Psychology
concepts.description: Wholly-other otherness; infinite task of integration; gender and psychology

concepts.title: The Animus as Pure Negation: Spirit and Opposition
concepts.description: Not connection to unconscious but negation of anima's natural flow. Establishes ego and reality as external

concepts.title: Soul Versus Psyche: Technical Distinction
concepts.description: Psyche = organism behavior; Soul = objective reality beyond personal. Conflation prevents genuine psychology

concepts.title: Killing Versus Murder: Psychology of Deliberate Harm
concepts.description: Psychological killing (soul-driven, independent of empirical causation) vs. pathological killing (trauma/passion driven)

concepts.title: Sacrifice as Soul-Making: Primordial and Necessary
concepts.description: Ritual killing as soul's free invention. Homo necans. Sacred experience through death-blow and blood

concepts.title: First-Order vs. Second-Order Killings: Soul's Historical Differentiation
concepts.description: Ritual sacrifice (archaic) vs. philosophical rejection (modernity). Repression as consequence not resolution

concepts.title: Separation of Heaven and Earth: Sacrifice as Cosmological Act
concepts.description: Sacrifice as cosmic structure separating differentiated from undifferentiated. Self-killing-into-being. Personhood emerges through I/Thou encounter

concepts.title: Necessity of Regularly Repeated Sacrifice: Entropy and Soul-Maintenance
concepts.description: Sacrifice must repeat perpetually. Cosmos requires continual reinforcement. Child must be repeatedly killed

concepts.title: Christianity: Preservation of the Child—Civilizational Inversion
concepts.description: Unprecedented: preserve innocence, sanctify the child. Modernity locked in child-consciousness, unable to complete initiation

concepts.title: Emancipation From Sacrifice: Second-Order Killings and Modernity
concepts.description: Modernity's vehement rejection; cost of repression; soul still needs killing; manifests as terrorism, institutional violence

concepts.title: Money as Modern God: Virtual Reality and Incarnationless Faith
concepts.description: Money as manifestation of Christian non-incarnate God. Pure abstraction, faith-based value. Perfect expression of child-consciousness logic

concepts.title: Psychology's Platonism Problem: Privileging Forms Over Manifestation
concepts.description: Psychology inherits Platonism: ideal forms more real than actual manifestation. Gap between theory and soul's actual operations

concepts.title: Direct Engagement With Soul vs. Therapeutic Evasion
concepts.description: Therapy and psychology incompatible. Help as prevention of encounter. Therapeutic paradox at heart of contemporary psychology

concepts.title: Hillman vs. Gigerenzer: Imaginal Psychology and Concrete Manifestation
concepts.description: Hillman's imagination as redemptive vs. Gigerenzer's manifestation in history. Living tension

concepts.title: Consciousness and Reality: How Stance Constitutes World
concepts.description: Stance generates reality. Same events, different stances, different ontological status. District-attorney consciousness generates different world than fairytale consciousness

concepts.title: Christ as Final Sacrifice and Unresolved Paradox
concepts.description: Christianity abolishes sacrifice while centering on it. Sublimation through spiritualization. Unresolved in unconscious, erupting as modern violence

concepts.title: Wolfgang Gigerenzer: Biographical and Intellectual Background
concepts.description: Practicing analyst, 30+ years theoretical work. Development from Jung toward radical critique

concepts.title: Burkert's Homo Necans: Killing as Foundational to Humanity
concepts.description: Anthropological evidence. Hunting 95-99% of human history. Continuous with ritual sacrifice

concepts.title: Girard's Scapegoat Mechanism: Sacrifice and Social Order
concepts.description: Collective killing as origin of sacred and culture. Convergence with Burkert; Gigerenzer's engagement and divergence

concepts.title: Firstborn Sacrifice: Archetypal Practice and Consciousness Transformation
concepts.description: Historical documentation across cultures. Psychological meaning of targeting firstborn. Modernity's reversal and consequences

concepts.title: Islamic Terrorism: Repressed Sacrificial Consciousness Manifest
concepts.description: Psychological phenomenon not merely political. Explicit sacrifice language. Manifestation without ritual container

concepts.title: Archetypal Psychology Hub
concepts.description: Gigerenzer critiques how Jungian schools domesticated Jung's insight

concepts.title: Trauma and Somatic Psychology Hub
concepts.description: Different domain (trauma healing); Gigerenzer concerns soul-making through necessary violence

concepts.title: Disease Susceptibility
concepts.description: add sympathetic/parasympathetic disease patterns

concepts.title: Hierarchy of Basic Needs
concepts.description: Five levels with prepotency (urgency) not sequence; deficiency at any level produces specific pathology; satisfaction enables transcendence to higher pursuits

concepts.title: Deficiency Motivation vs. Growth Motivation
concepts.description: Two fundamentally different kinds of motivation operating by opposite principles; D-motivation = tension-reduction, B-motivation = tension-expansion; twelve key differences

concepts.title: Self-Actualization: Ongoing Actualization of Potentials
concepts.description: Not an endpoint but continuous unfolding; growth through delight; idiosyncratic expression; autonomy requirement; the reversal from deficiency motivation

concepts.title: The Eight Characteristics of Self-Actualizing People
concepts.description: Superior perception of reality, increased acceptance, spontaneity, problem-centering, detachment with privacy, autonomy, emotional richness, higher frequency of peak experiences

title: Perception and Experience: B-Cognition and Peak Experiences

concepts.title: B-Cognition and D-Cognition: Two Modes of Perceiving Reality
concepts.description: D-cognition filters through need; B-cognition perceives without the filter; characteristics of each mode; why psychology missed B-cognition

concepts.title: Dangers of Being-Cognition: Why Clarity Can Paralyze
concepts.description: Eight specific ways full perception can induce inaction: paralysis, dissolution of conviction, ego-boundary dissolution, overwhelming compassion, consequence-awareness, meaning-relativism, fear of corruption, loss of illusions

concepts.title: Peak Experiences: Moments of Acute Identity and Being-Cognition
concepts.description: Moments of acute aliveness where ego dissolves, opposites reconcile, time distorts, self-other boundary dissolves

concepts.title: Both/And Thinking: The Capacity to Hold Contradiction
concepts.description: Capacity to perceive contradictory truths simultaneously without collapsing into either/or; emerges from B-cognition and defensive relaxation

concepts.title: B-Values: What Being Reveals About Itself
concepts.description: Intrinsic values emerging when deficiency needs met: wholeness, perfection, aliveness, beauty, truth, goodness, simplicity, playfulness, autonomy, richness, self-actualization, transcendence

concepts.title: Authentic vs. Inauthentic Values
concepts.description: Authentic values emerge from genuine nature; inauthentic values introjected from external sources and maintained through shame; the felt-sense test

concepts.title: The Intrinsic Conscience vs. Authoritarian Superego
concepts.description: Superego as internalized authority; intrinsic conscience as alignment with own values emerging when esteem stable; shift from obligation to authentic commitment

concepts.title: Defense and Growth: The Choice Between Safety and Becoming
concepts.description: Fundamental tension between defensive pull and growth pull; four-valence structure; conditions permitting shift to growth dominance

concepts.title: Healthy Regression and Coasting Values: The Necessity of Rest
concepts.description: Growth isn't constant; healthy regression is consolidation and integration; coasting values are the choice for sustainable living

concepts.title: Transcendence of Environment: Freedom Through Inner Criteria
concepts.description: Capacity to maintain values based on internal criteria rather than external circumstances; not spiritual transcendence but psychological independence

concepts.title: Autonomy and Resistance to Enculturation: Freedom Within Culture
concepts.description: Simultaneous acceptance and detachment from culture; not rebellion but selective engagement; emerges when basic needs stable

concepts.title: Healthy Anger: The Emotion That Accomplishes
concepts.description: Anger that's proportional, communicative, goal-directed, controlled, produces results; transformation from cathartic to effective with development

concepts.title: Healthy Unconscious: The Source That Isn't Diseased
concepts.description: Healthy unconscious contains creative potential, somatic wisdom, pattern recognition, emotional depth, authentic impulse; primary-secondary process integration

concepts.title: Neural Networks and Connectionist Brain Architecture
concepts.description: Individual and civilizational parallel processing; male-female architecture differences; connectionist superiority for complex problem-solving; implications for group cognition and institutional design

concepts.title: Endorphin Anesthesia and Comfort-Seeking in Decline
concepts.description: Neurochemical mechanism of learned helplessness; endorphin-mediated comfort-seeking as system shutdown; entertainment excess and drug use as anesthesia; how neurochemistry locks civilizations in decline

concepts.title: Parentage as Operational Mindset Source
concepts.description: Childhood attachment determines baseline psychological capacity for operational confidence in decision-making under uncertainty. Secure attachment creates confidence; insecure creates hesitation and need for validation. Three components: baseline decision-making confidence, comfort with incomplete information, willingness to handle failure. Psychological foundation formed early, difficult to retrain.

concepts.title: Self-Education as Permission-Seeking
concepts.description: Self-education functions as knowledge acquisition AND psychological permission-seeking—proving to gatekeepers that you're ready for advancement. Paradox: insecure permission-seeking (extensive study) often appears more committed to gatekeepers than secure confidence (assuming readiness). Voracious learning serves as preparation and permission signal simultaneously.

concepts.title: Hardwork Paradox
concepts.description: Paradox where someone maintains narrative of intense hardwork while structural reality becomes increasingly passive (passive income, delegated execution). Narrative doesn't update to match structure; it intensifies. Reveals hardwork narrative serves psychological function independent of accuracy—maintains nervous system safety. Narrative persists because nervous system learned in scarcity that "security = personal effort."

concepts.title: Purpose Collapse as Existential Trap
concepts.description: When someone organizes entire identity around achieving primary goal (financial independence, dominance, status), they risk existential collapse once goal achieved. Goal was not generating meaning but avoiding question of meaning. Once achieved, meaning evaporates. Person enters desperate search for replacement purposes (philanthropy, reform, legacy-building) because nervous system requires striving to feel alive.

title: Subsection 3a: Ideological Shame and Affect Socialization (3 pages)

concepts.title: Ideological Scripts and Affect Socialization
concepts.description: How shame functions as socialization mechanism in cultural contexts; transmission through family systems and institutional structures; affect internalization as identity formation

concepts.title: Humanistic vs. Normative Ideological Orientations
concepts.description: Two competing shame-based value systems: humanistic (acceptance of human limitation and vulnerability) vs. normative (demand for conformity and control); Kaufman maps which shame orientation a culture installs

concepts.title: The Bifurcation of Affects: How Hierarchy Splits the Emotional Spectrum
concepts.description: In hierarchical systems, shame becomes a splitting mechanism that divides the healthy affect spectrum into acceptable and prohibited ranges; lower-status individuals internalize bifurcated affect ranges as identity

title: Subsection 3b: Ideological Architecture & Collective Governing Scenes (4 pages)

concepts.title: Shame, Anger, Contempt, and the Architecture of Hatred
concepts.description: Hatred as collective expression organized through shame-anger-contempt cascade; how shame becomes weaponized at cultural level; contempt as defense against shared shame

concepts.title: Governing Cultural Scenes and Collective Identity Symbols
concepts.description: Cultural organizing principles (founding atrocities, collective humiliations) become institutional through ceremonies, education, political rhetoric; how societies magnify and perpetuate governing scenes across generations

concepts.title: Minority Identity and Shame Scenes: Political Shame at the Collective Level
concepts.description: How minority group identity becomes organized around collective shame scenes; distinguishing between shame-driven activism (reactive, magnified) and resource-driven justice work (generative, specific)

concepts.title: Contempt as Defensive Shame Strategy vs. Genuine Pride
concepts.description: Contempt as internalized shame turned outward; how distinguishing defensive contempt from genuine pride reveals the underlying shame architecture in individuals and cultures

title: Subsection 3c: Memory, Trauma Magnification, and Scene Intrusion (6 pages)

concepts.title: Trauma Magnification: How Events Become Traumatic
concepts.description: Not all difficult experiences become traumatic; traumatic status emerges through repetition with affect intensification; the role of narrative rehearsal in magnification; individual and cultural examples

concepts.title: Survivor Shame: The Bittersweet Joy of Rescue
concepts.description: Distinct from PTSD; shame arising from having survived when others didn't; paradox of relief + guilt + identity confusion; why survivor shame responds differently to therapeutic approaches than PTSD symptoms

concepts.title: Memory Magnification and the Construction of Trauma
concepts.description: How therapeutic rehearsal can inadvertently magnify memory; distinction between retrieving actual memories and constructing narratives through therapeutic process; the role of certainty in cementing constructed narratives

concepts.title: Memory Retrieval and Recovery: Principles and Practice
concepts.description: Distinctive discontinuity principle applied to memory access; memory as reconstruction not retrieval; how to work with fragmented memories without forcing coherence; recovery as integration not certainty

concepts.title: Scene Intrusion in Posttraumatic Stress Disorder
concepts.description: Involuntary reactivation of governing scenes; scene as present-tense experience rather than memory; why ordinary memory techniques don't work for scene intrusion; three channels of reactivation (sensory, auditory, affect-silent)

concepts.title: Three Channels of Scene Reactivation: Sensory, Auditory, and Affect-Silent Pathways
concepts.description: Scenes activate not through conscious thought but through sensory triggers, sounds, or pure nervous system states; mapping which channel is active determines which intervention strategy will work

title: Subsection 3d: Memory Storage, Retrieval, and Pre-Verbal Encoding (3 pages)

concepts.title: The Relationship Between Storage and Retrieval in Memory: Storage Is Not Filing; Retrieval Is Not Finding
concepts.description: Memory is stored in fragments across multiple neural systems (amygdala fear, sensory cortex images, hippocampus temporal context); retrieval is always reconstruction; consciousness is the illusion of unified memory

concepts.title: Governing Scenes: From Individual Memory to Collective Culture
concepts.description: Private governing scenes become public when magnified and institutionalized; how individuals' trauma-shaped responses become cultural organizing principles; magnification through repetition, commemoration, and institutional embedding

concepts.title: Recovering Early Memories: Accessing Distinctive Discontinuity
concepts.description: Pre-verbal trauma encoded in nervous system before language develops; distinctive discontinuity provides entry points (somatic, sensory, affective-silent); healing early trauma doesn't require narrative recovery, only nervous system recalibration

title: Subsection 3e: Intergenerational Transmission, Memory Standards, and Scene Transformation (3 pages)

concepts.title: Genocide, War, and the Reenactment of Governing Scenes
concepts.description: Children of survivors inherit parent's trauma-shaped nervous system calibration; inherited scenes reactivate in new situations; perpetration cycles as nervous system inheritance not conscious choice; breaking cycles requires recognizing and recalibrating inherited patterns

concepts.title: The Real/False Memory Question: Psychology vs. Law
concepts.description: Memory can be psychologically real (organizing nervous system), legally inadmissible (no corroboration), and epistemically uncertain (indistinguishable from false) simultaneously; three truth standards operating independently; the impossibility of satisfying all three standards at once

concepts.title: Scene Transformation at Individual and Cultural Level: Moving Scenes From Present to Past Tense
concepts.description: Governing scenes can be recontextualized (not erased or suppressed) through deliberate activation in safety; transformation mechanism: accessibility → activation in safety → narrative integration → nervous system recalibration → identity expansion; applies to individual therapy and collective cultural work

concepts.title: Real Self vs. Co-dependent Self
concepts.description: Whitfield's foundational split: the buried authentic child versus the protective false persona that replaced it; how to tell them apart by felt sense and behavioral signature

concepts.title: Co-dependence as Clinical Condition
concepts.description: Whitfield's formal account: co-dependence as the co-dependent self systematically overriding the Real Self; clinical picture distinguishing it from pop-psych usage

concepts.title: Recovery Stages Framework
concepts.description: Whitfield's staged model: survival → emerging → transforming → spirituality; each stage has distinct tasks and developmental prerequisites; cannot be rushed without producing bypass

concepts.title: Transformation in Recovery
concepts.description: The hinge stage between co-dependent functioning and Real Self access; what structurally changes during transformation; the specific shift from co-dependent self to authentic self in operation

concepts.title: Core Recovery Issues
concepts.description: Whitfield's 20-item hierarchy of the specific developmental deficits co-dependence leaves behind; what must be recovered and in what sequence

concepts.title: Human Needs Hierarchy in Recovery
concepts.description: Whitfield's mapping of recovery tasks onto Maslow's framework; need 20 (unconditional love) as the final and structurally hardest recovery task; three-column alignment with consciousness levels

concepts.title: Boundary Setting in Recovery
concepts.description: Whitfield: boundaries as the recovered self's primary structural tool; the difference between walls and limits; why co-dependent self confuses protection with isolation

title: Clinical Tools: Feeling and Self-Disclosure (3 pages)

concepts.title: Emotional Awareness and Feeling Identification
concepts.description: Whitfield's three-level model for recovering feeling capacity after emotional numbing; the share-check-share protocol for graduated disclosure without retraumatization

concepts.title: Risking and Storytelling as Healing
concepts.description: Self-disclosure to safe others as primary recovery technology; hero/heroine versus martyr/victim narrative stance; Table 12's eight parent-protection blocks; Campbell's three-part story structure applied clinically

concepts.title: Grief as Labor and Healing
concepts.description: Whitfield's account of grief as active work rather than passive mourning; the specific grief tasks that unlock the recovery stages; why grief that is avoided accumulates compound pressure

concepts.title: Shame Cycle and Approach-Avoidance
concepts.description: The self-reinforcing loop where shame drives approach-then-retreat behavior; why intimacy triggers the very withdrawal that makes it more needed; Whitfield's account of the cycle's self-perpetuation

concepts.title: Repetition Compulsion and Age Regression
concepts.description: Why adults re-enact childhood wounds; how emotional age regression locks the child's state inside adult behaviors; Whitfield's clinical account of how adults collapse to earlier developmental levels under stress

concepts.title: Parental Conditions in Dysfunctional Families
concepts.description: The specific parenting failures — abuse, neglect, enmeshment, role reversal, emotional unavailability — that disable the child's development; Whitfield's framework for mapping which conditions produced which deficits

title: Observer Self and Spiritual Apex (2 pages)

concepts.title: The Observer Self
concepts.description: Deikman and Whitfield: the featureless witnessing capacity that cannot itself be observed; late-stage developmental achievement requiring prior ego-building for stability; bridge between psychological recovery and spiritual territory

concepts.title: Spirituality in Recovery
concepts.description: Whitfield's last stage that is also the ground; the paradox of spirituality as both final destination and always-present reality; seven levels of the Child Within; serenity as inherent state (Da Free John); unconditional love as the hardest final need

concepts.title: Identity Disruption Under Coercive Pressure
concepts.description: Identity as dynamic system reorganizing around whatever dominates reality-input stream; 3 coercive routes (isolation, cognitive depletion, deliberate disorientation); Cameron's total disruption as evidence that complete erasure creates void not receptive field

concepts.title: Trauma Bonding Under Manufactured Dependency
concepts.description: Intermittent reinforcement mechanism; does not require prior relationship; perceived threat + small kindness + sole-source safety all deliberately manufacturable; persists beyond coercive environment

concepts.title: Learned Helplessness in Captivity Conditions
concepts.description: Accurate inference that effort doesn't affect outcomes; threshold effect; two producing conditions (uncontrollability + unpredictability); staged development; generalizes beyond captivity

concepts.title: False Confession Psychology
concepts.description: 3 types: coerced-compliant, coerced-internalized, voluntary; 4.5× false confession rate under sleep deprivation; conditions producing confession also degrade reliability of content; 15-25% of wrongful convictions (Innocence Project)

concepts.title: Suggestibility Under Extreme Stress
concepts.description: Predictable neurological output of physiological conditions; 3 degrading systems + social isolation amplification; dose-response curve: moderate → high → extreme (qualitative shift at transmarginal threshold); compliance vs. epistemic suggestibility distinction

concepts.title: Cult Exit Psychology — Why Leaving Is Not Enough
concepts.description: 5 simultaneous losses at exit; APA deprogramming controversy; floating state (between-frameworks dissociation); peak re-recruitment vulnerability at exit; conditions for successful recovery

concepts.title: The Ordinary Person Thesis: Who Coercive Persuasion Actually Works On
concepts.description: Conditions predict outcomes, not individual pathology; Hebb/Milgram/Asch/Mindszenty/Korean War POW evidence; self-protective illusion as primary epistemic obstacle; framing page for entire Dimsdale cluster
